panels.title: Active Sessions
panels.description: Current active queries. Sustained growth usually means query latency or app-side concurrency pressure.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*)::double precision AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' AND state = 'active';

panels.title: Lock Waiters
panels.description: Sessions waiting on locks. Non-zero for more than a brief spike indicates blocking or long transactions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*)::double precision AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' AND wait_event_type = 'Lock';

panels.title: DB Size
panels.description: Current database size. Track trend for storage planning and sudden jumps from bulk writes or bloat.

panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: Buffer cache effectiveness. Consistently below ~95% often means more disk reads and slower query response.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100 * SUM(blks_hit)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(blks_hit + blks_read), 0), 2)::double precision AS value FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = '${db}';

panels.title: Sessions by State
panels.description: Breakdown of backend states (active, idle, idle in transaction). High idle-in-transaction is a red flag.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(state, 'unknown') AS state, count(*) AS sessions FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY sessions DESC;

panels.title: Current Wait Events
panels.description: Where sessions are waiting right now. Dominant lock or I/O waits point directly to contention hotspots.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(wait_event_type, 'CPU') AS wait_type, COALESCE(wait_event, 'running') AS wait_event, count(*) AS sessions FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY sessions DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Database Counters Snapshot
panels.description: Core DB counters. Watch deadlocks/temp files/temp bytes for regressions and memory/work_mem pressure.

targets.rawSql: SELECT numbackends, xact_commit, xact_rollback, blks_read, blks_hit, tup_returned, tup_fetched, tup_inserted, tup_updated, tup_deleted, deadlocks, temp_files, pg_size_pretty(temp_bytes) AS temp_bytes FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = '${db}';

panels.title: Top SQL by Total Time (pg_stat_statements)
panels.description: Heaviest statements by cumulative execution time. Prioritize high total_ms plus high mean_ms or io_wait_ms.

targets.rawSql: SELECT queryid::text AS query_id, calls, ROUND(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS total_ms, ROUND(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS mean_ms, ROUND((blk_read_time + blk_write_time)::numeric, 2) AS io_wait_ms, rows, LEFT(REGEXP_REPLACE(query, '\s+', ' ', 'g'), 180) AS query_sample FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Top Tables by Buffer Read Pressure
panels.description: Tables with most physical reads. High heap_blks_read and low hit% suggest index, cache, or query-shape issues.

targets.rawSql: SELECT relname AS table_name, heap_blks_read, heap_blks_hit, ROUND(100 * heap_blks_hit::numeric / NULLIF(heap_blks_hit + heap_blks_read, 0), 2) AS heap_hit_pct, idx_blks_read, idx_blks_hit FROM pg_statio_user_tables ORDER BY heap_blks_read DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Checkpoint / Writer Activity
panels.description: Background writer/checkpoint load. Rising checkpoint times or backend fsyncs indicate write I/O stress.

targets.rawSql: SELECT checkpoints_timed, checkpoints_req, checkpoint_write_time, checkpoint_sync_time, buffers_checkpoint, buffers_clean, maxwritten_clean, buffers_backend, buffers_backend_fsync, buffers_alloc FROM pg_stat_bgwriter;

panels.title: Blocking Tree (Who Blocks Whom)
panels.description: Shows exactly which session is blocked by which blocker. If rows persist beyond short spikes, users are waiting and throughput is being throttled by lock contention.

targets.rawSql: SELECT blocked.pid AS blocked_pid, blocked.usename AS blocked_user, age(now(), blocked.query_start) AS blocked_for, blocker.pid AS blocker_pid, blocker.usename AS blocker_user, age(now(), blocker.query_start) AS blocker_running_for, LEFT(blocked.query, 140) AS blocked_query, LEFT(blocker.query, 140) AS blocker_query FROM pg_stat_activity blocked JOIN pg_stat_activity blocker ON blocker.pid = ANY(pg_blocking_pids(blocked.pid)) WHERE blocked.datname = '${db}' ORDER BY blocked_for DESC;

panels.title: Long-Running Transactions
panels.description: Active transactions sorted by age. Older transactions delay vacuum cleanup and can hold locks longer than expected.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pid, usename, state, age(now(), xact_start) AS tx_age, wait_event_type, wait_event, LEFT(query, 160) AS query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' AND xact_start IS NOT NULL AND state <> 'idle' ORDER BY xact_start ASC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Idle-in-Transaction Offenders
panels.description: Sessions that opened a transaction and then went idle. These are high-risk for hidden lock contention and transaction bloat.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pid, usename, age(now(), xact_start) AS idle_tx_age, LEFT(query, 160) AS query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = '${db}' AND state = 'idle in transaction' ORDER BY xact_start ASC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Vacuum Debt (Dead Tuple Pressure)
panels.description: Tables accumulating dead rows. High dead_pct or large dead tuple counts indicate cleanup debt and possible performance decay.

targets.rawSql: SELECT schemaname || '.' || relname AS table_name, n_live_tup, n_dead_tup, ROUND(100 * n_dead_tup::numeric / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0), 2) AS dead_pct, last_autovacuum, last_vacuum FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY n_dead_tup DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Autovacuum Staleness
panels.description: How long each table has gone without vacuum maintenance. Long staleness with rising dead tuples usually predicts write/read slowdown.

targets.rawSql: SELECT schemaname || '.' || relname AS table_name, now() - COALESCE(last_autovacuum, last_vacuum, 'epoch'::timestamp) AS since_maintenance, n_dead_tup, autovacuum_count, vacuum_count FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY since_maintenance DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Top SQL by I/O Wait Ratio
panels.description: Queries with the largest share of execution time spent waiting on disk I/O. High io_pct points to storage/index/query-shape bottlenecks.

targets.rawSql: SELECT queryid::text AS query_id, calls, ROUND(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS total_ms, ROUND((blk_read_time + blk_write_time)::numeric, 2) AS io_ms, ROUND((100::numeric * (blk_read_time + blk_write_time)::numeric) / NULLIF(total_exec_time::numeric, 0), 2) AS io_pct, LEFT(REGEXP_REPLACE(query, '\s+', ' ', 'g'), 180) AS query_sample FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE calls > 20 ORDER BY io_pct DESC, total_exec_time DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Top SQL by Temp Spill
panels.description: Statements spilling to temporary files. Large temp_mb usually means memory pressure (sort/hash spill) and extra disk churn.

targets.rawSql: SELECT queryid::text AS query_id, calls, ROUND(((temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written) * 8) / 1024.0, 2) AS temp_mb, ROUND(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS mean_ms, LEFT(REGEXP_REPLACE(query, '\s+', ' ', 'g'), 180) AS query_sample FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE (temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written) > 0 ORDER BY (temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written) DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Timescale Compression Backlog
panels.description: Old chunks that should be compressed but are still uncompressed. A growing backlog means higher storage cost and slower historical scans.

targets.rawSql: SELECT hypertable_schema || '.' || hypertable_name AS hypertable, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE range_end < now() - interval '7 days') AS old_chunks, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE range_end < now() - interval '7 days' AND NOT is_compressed) AS old_uncompressed, ROUND(100 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE range_end < now() - interval '7 days' AND is_compressed)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE range_end < now() - interval '7 days'), 0), 2) AS compression_pct FROM timescaledb_information.chunks GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY old_uncompressed DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Timescale Job Health (Failures + Overdue)
panels.description: Background job health for compression/retention/maintenance. Failures or long overdue_sec values mean policy drift and mounting maintenance debt.

targets.rawSql: SELECT j.job_id, j.application_name, js.last_run_status, js.total_failures, js.last_run_started_at, js.last_successful_finish, js.next_start, GREATEST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - js.next_start)), 0)::bigint AS overdue_sec FROM timescaledb_information.jobs j LEFT JOIN timescaledb_information.job_stats js ON js.job_id = j.job_id ORDER BY js.total_failures DESC NULLS LAST, overdue_sec DESC LIMIT ${topn};

panels.title: Deadlock / ERROR Log Rate (Loki)
panels.description: Live deadlock and ERROR/FATAL rates from Postgres logs. Sustained deadlocks above zero or rising ERROR rate is an immediate reliability warning.

list.name: db
list.label: Database
list.query: SELECT datname FROM pg_database WHERE datistemplate = false ORDER BY datname
